title: Lesson 26 — The Flatterer

words.example: The young star basked in the adulation of the crowd.

words.definition: special honor or respect shown publicly
words.example: The film pays homage to the directors who inspired it.
The rescue crew earned the plaudits of the whole town.

words.definition: a person who flatters others to gain favor
words.example: The manager kept a toady on hand to agree with everything he said.

words.definition: to persuade someone by flattery or coaxing
words.example: She wheedled an extension out of her lenient professor.

words.definition: flattering or pleasing speech used to persuade
words.example: No blandishment could change the auditor's mind.

words.definition: excessively flattering or ingratiating in an oily way
words.example: The unctuous salesman made everyone's skin crawl.

words.definition: complimentary to an excessive, insincere degree
words.example: His fulsome praise felt more like mockery than tribute.

words.definition: to bring oneself into favor with someone by deliberate effort
words.example: He tried to ingratiate himself with the new boss.

words.definition: to act in an abjectly submissive way to win favor or forgiveness
words.example: He groveled for a second chance, promising anything.

words.definition: to praise excessively, often insincerely, to please or gain advantage
words.example: She flattered the critic hoping for a kind review.

words.definition: deferential respect, or a gesture such as a bow expressing it
words.example: The courtiers made a deep obeisance as the queen passed.

story: The new courtier quickly mastered the art of flattery. Each morning he greeted the king with [[homage|homage]] so extravagant it bordered on [[adulation|adulation]], showering him with [[plaudits|plaudits]] for decisions no one else dared praise. A practiced [[toady|toady]], he would [[wheedle|wheedle]] favors with soft [[blandishment|blandishments]], his [[unctuous|unctuous]] compliments as [[fulsome|fulsome]] as they were false. To [[ingratiate|ingratiate]] himself further he would [[grovel|grovel]] before the throne and [[flatter|flatter]] any noble who seemed likely to rise, performing each bow with a deep [[obeisance|obeisance]] that made the older courtiers wince.
